sections.title: A: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: The (1) ...... (EXPLORE) phase of any new project is often the most exciting, but it also requires a great deal of (2) ...... (TOLERATE) for ambiguity and unexpected challenges.

Young people entering the job market today face an (3) ...... (INCREASE) competitive environment in which academic (4) ...... (ACHIEVE) alone may not be sufficient to stand out from other (5) ...... (APPLY).
The government's (6) ...... (RESPONSIBLE) for tackling climate change is, (7) ...... (ARGUE), greater than that of any individual citizen, given its power to implement policy on a national scale.
The (8) ...... (DRAMA) rise in the use of social media over the past decade has led to serious (9) ...... (QUESTION) about its long-term effects on mental health.
It is widely (10) ...... (RECOGNISE) that creativity and critical thinking are among the most valuable skills an employee can bring to the workplace.

sections.title: B: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
There's absolutely no value in arguing with him – he'll never change his mind. **POINT**
There ………………………………………………………………… arguing with him – he'll never change his mind.

It would be a good idea to see the film before you read the book. **WORTH**
The film ………………………………………………………………… before you read the book.

I regret not accepting the job offer when it was made. **WISH**
I ………………………………………………………………… the job offer when it was made.

You may leave the examination room early, but only if you have finished all the questions. **PROVIDED**
You may leave the examination room early ………………………………………………………………… all the questions.

The politician said it was not true that he had accepted money from the company. **DENIED**
The politician ………………………………………………………………… money from the company.

You can only access the members' area if you register on the website first. **UNLESS**
You cannot access the members' area ………………………………………………………………… on the website.
items.answer: unless you register / unless you have registered

items.sentence:
The new policy resulted in significant changes to the recruitment process. **BROUGHT**
The new policy ………………………………………………………………… in the recruitment process.

I would prefer to take the train instead of driving all the way to Edinburgh. **RATHER**
I would ………………………………………………………………… drive all the way to Edinburgh.

sections.title: C: Idioms / Collocations
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: It took the committee weeks to get to the ……………………………………… of the problem, but eventually a solution was found.
You need to keep your ……………………………………… on the ball during the final round – there's no room for mistakes.
He's been burning the ……………………………………… at both ends all month and now he's completely exhausted.
The invitation came completely out of the ……………………………………… – nobody had expected to be contacted after so many years.
Don't count your ……………………………………… before they hatch – we haven't signed the contract yet.
The unexpected system failure threw a ……………………………………… in the works and delayed the product launch by two weeks.
I know it's an unpleasant conversation to have, but you just have to bite the ……………………………………… and get it over with.
She's very good at thinking on her ……………………………………… – she always knows exactly what to say in a tricky situation.
I really can't **put / hold / stand** up with his constant interruptions any longer.
The organisers decided to **call / cut / turn** off the outdoor concert due to the bad weather.
She managed to **pull / carry / come** through the difficult operation despite the complications.
The new regulations will **come / go / fall** into effect from the start of next month.
He tried to **brush / wipe / sweep** the incident under the carpet, but a journalist soon found out about it.
We need to **draw / pick / make** up a new agreement with the suppliers before the deadline.
The investigation **turned / brought / showed** up some surprising new evidence that changed the course of the trial.
It took her a while to **get / come / move** over the disappointment of not being selected for the team.
The exhibition attracted widespread ...... from critics and audiences alike.
The scientists failed to reach a ...... on whether the findings were conclusive enough to publish.
The rescue team worked ...... the clock to find the survivors buried beneath the rubble.
Her ...... to detail is what makes her an exceptional editor.
It was ...... luck that he happened to be nearby when the accident occurred.
The documentary managed to shed ...... on a largely forgotten chapter of history.
You should really ...... up on your presentation skills before the meeting with the board.
I'm not sure I can justify ...... so much money on a new computer right now.
